BASED ON RADAR, VIDEO, AND EYEWITNESS REPORTS, NWS MELBOURNE CAN CONFIRM A BRIEF, WEAK EF-0 LANDSPOUT TORNADO OCCURRED IN VERO LAKES ESTATES AT 412 PM ON SATURDAY FEBRUARY 6.

PUBLIC REPORTS FENCE DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF 79TH STREET AND 100 AVE IN VERO LAKES ESTATES, ALONG WITH SOME BACKYARD ITEMS THROWN ABOUT. POSSIBLE BRIEF TORNADO MAY HAVE TOUCHED DOWN.

NWS MELBOURNE CAN CONFIRM STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS, ESTIMATED BETWEEN 65-70 MPH, OCCURRED AT THE COCOA BAY MOBILE HOME COMMUNITY AROUND 235 PM. BASED ON INFORMATION PROVIDED BY BREVARD COUNTY EM, COCOA FIRE DEPARTMENT, LOCAL NEWS MEDIA, AND EYE WITNESS REPORTS THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OCCURRED TO A COUPLE OF HOMES THAT HAD SECTIONS OF THEIR ROOFS UPLIFTED. ADDITIONAL DAMAGE IN THE COMMUNITY ALONG COCOA BAY BLVD. INCLUDED DOWNED FENCES, DAMAGED SCREENS/AWNINGS, AND LARGE BRANCHES DOWNED.
